title: CPE C2 Proficiency Reading & Use of English - Practice Test 6 (Book 3)
source: CPE Practice Tests 3, Virginia Evans, Express Publishing

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-8, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Part 1: Cloze Texts

School Trips

Each term two days were allocated for day trips, which we all looked forward to as a (1) ______ break from the school timetable. Nine times out of ten it would rain, but as children we were (2) ______ to the weather, exhilarated by our day of freedom. Teachers, on the other hand, would (3) ______ together under umbrellas, occasionally reprimanding those pupils whose behaviour was bordering on the unacceptable. On one (4) ______ , the weather was so bad that the headmaster had no choice but to cancel the outing. On seeing our faces drop he must have felt a (5) ______ of guilt because we were consequently called to the assembly hall where a morning of fun and games awaited us. Beyond a (6) ______ of a doubt, it was the most enjoyable 'school trip' we ever had.

Hail

Hail is a (7) ______ of spring and summer storms, and can sometimes be large enough to cause considerable (8) ______ to buildings, cars and crops.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: Gap (1):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Cụm từ cố định (collocation) 'a welcome break' nghĩa là 'một sự nghỉ ngơi/ngắt quãng rất đáng mừng/dễ chịu'. Bẫy: 'happy' (hạnh phúc), 'gleeful' (hân hoan/hớn hở), 'relaxed' (thư thái) không dùng để tạo thành cụm từ cố định chuẩn với 'break' trong ngữ cảnh này. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: all looked forward to as a welcome break
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (2):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Tính từ 'oblivious' đi kèm giới từ 'to' nghĩa là 'không để ý đến / quên hết xung quanh / mù tịt': 'oblivious to the weather' (không bận tâm/để ý đến thời tiết xấu). Bẫy: 'ignorant of' là thiếu hiểu biết/dốt nát về, 'unaware of' là không nhận thức được, 'regardless of' nghĩa là bất chấp/bất kể cái gì. Chuyên đề: Prepositions after adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: as children we were oblivious to the weather
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (3):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Động từ 'huddle' chỉ việc đứng túm tụm lại/co ro bên nhau (thường để tránh rét hoặc che mưa): 'huddle together under umbrellas'. Bẫy: 'flock' là tụ tập đông như bầy đàn (thường đi với giới từ to/into), 'cuddle' là ôm ấp âu yếm, 'meet' là gặp gỡ thông thường. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — Verbs.
questions.answerLocation: Teachers, on the other hand, would huddle together under umbrellas
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (4):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Cụm từ cố định 'On one occasion' nghĩa là 'vào một dịp / một lần nọ'. Bẫy: 'on one time' không đúng ngữ pháp, 'in the event' là trong trường hợp, 'at one moment' là vào một khoảnh khắc nào đó. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: On one occasion, the weather was so bad
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (5):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Cụm từ cố định 'a twinge of guilt' chỉ một cơn nhói lên / cảm giác cắn rứt lương tâm thoáng qua: 'felt a twinge of guilt'. Bẫy: 'sensation' là cảm giác thể chất/tri giác, 'pain' là cơn đau kéo dài, 'sting' là vết châm/chích đau điếng. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: he must have felt a twinge of guilt
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (6):
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Thành ngữ cố định 'beyond a shadow of a doubt' nghĩa là 'không một chút nghi ngờ nào / hoàn toàn chắc chắn'. Bẫy: Các từ 'shade' (bóng râm), 'trace' (dấu vết), 'piece' (mảnh) không thể thay thế trong thành ngữ này. Chuyên đề: Idioms & Fixed expressions.
questions.answerLocation: Beyond a shadow of a doubt, it was the most enjoyable
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (7):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Danh từ 'feature' nghĩa là đặc điểm đặc trưng nổi bật: 'Hail is a feature of spring and summer storms' (Mưa đá là một đặc trưng của các cơn bão mùa xuân và mùa hè). Bẫy: 'highlight' là điểm nhấn nổi bật nhất của một chương trình, 'mainstay' là trụ cột chính, 'facet' là khía cạnh của một vấn đề. Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — Nouns. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: Hail is a feature of spring and summer storms
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (8):
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Danh từ không đếm được 'damage' đi kèm động từ 'cause' chỉ sự thiệt hại vật chất: 'cause considerable damage to buildings...' (gây ra thiệt hại đáng kể cho các tòa nhà...). Bẫy: 'demolition' là sự phá dỡ công trình xây dựng, 'disruption' là sự gián đoạn, 'desolation' là sự hoang tàn/tàn phá/cô độc. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: cause considerable damage to buildings, cars and crops.
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 9-16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Ancient Time Keeping

The Aztec and Mayan calendars were very SIMILAR. The Aztecs, (9) ______ , had a more primitive number system and consequently a (10) ______ exact way of calculating dates. A complicated system of two concurrent calendars existed, one marking the days and the (11) ______ the years. The (12) ______ was arranged on a 260-day cycle divided (13) ______ 20 periods and then subdivided again.

It was used as a religious calendar and the priests could thereby decide on important activities like going to war or building projects. The latter was based on the (14) ______ more familiar 365-day solar count. It was also divided and subdivided but into smaller periods than our own Julian calendar. Five days, (15) ______ were not represented at all, were set aside as (16) ______ time for festivities.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct word in each gap.

questions.instruction: Gap (9): The Aztecs, ______ , had a more primitive...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: HOWEVER (hoặc THOUGH). Trạng từ liên kết chỉ sự tương phản 'however' đứng giữa hai dấu phẩy dùng để đối lập hệ thống của người Aztec với lịch của người Maya ở câu trước. Chuyên đề: Discourse markers. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: The Aztecs, however, had a more primitive
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (10): consequently a ______ exact way...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: LESS. Trạng từ chỉ mức độ phủ định 'less' đứng trước tính từ 'exact' để diễn tả ý 'ít chính xác hơn' do hệ thống số đếm thô sơ của người Aztec. Chuyên đề: Determiners. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: consequently a less exact way of calculating
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (11): one marking the days and the ______ the years.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: OTHER. Cấu trúc đối lập song hành 'one... and the other...' để chỉ hai đối tượng khác nhau: một cái chỉ ngày và cái còn lại chỉ năm. Chuyên đề: Pronouns.
questions.answerLocation: one marking the days and the other the years.
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (12): The ______ was arranged on a 260-day cycle...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: FORMER. Đại từ xác định 'The former' (đối tượng được nhắc đến trước tiên - tức lịch đánh dấu ngày) đối lập với 'The latter' (đối tượng nhắc đến sau) ở phần tiếp theo của văn bản. Chuyên đề: Pronouns. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: The former was arranged on a 260-day cycle
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (13): divided ______ 20 periods...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: INTO. Cụm động từ bị động 'divided into' nghĩa là được chia thành bao nhiêu phần/giai đoạn. Chuyên đề: Prepositional verbs.
questions.answerLocation: divided into 20 periods and then subdivided
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (14): based on the ______ more familiar 365-day...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: FAR (hoặc MUCH). Trạng từ 'far' hoặc 'much' đứng trước cấu trúc so sánh hơn/so sánh tính từ 'more familiar' để tăng cường mức độ nhấn mạnh (quen thuộc hơn rất nhiều). Chuyên đề: Modifiers.
questions.answerLocation: based on the far more familiar 365-day
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (15): Five days, ______ were not represented at all...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WHICH. Đại từ quan hệ 'which' dẫn đầu một mệnh đề quan hệ không xác định (non-defining relative clause) bổ nghĩa cho cụm danh từ 'Five days' đứng trước. Chuyên đề: Relative clauses.
questions.answerLocation: Five days, which were not represented at all,
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (16): set aside as ______ time for festivities.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EXTRA (hoặc FREE / SPARE). Tính từ chỉ thời gian dôi dư ra ngoài lịch chính thức: 'set aside as extra time for festivities' (dành riêng ra như thời gian bổ sung cho các lễ hội). Chuyên đề: Vocabulary — Adjectives. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: set aside as extra time for festivities.
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 17-24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the space in the same line.
sections.passageHtml:
The Cutty Sark

This famous SAILING ship was built in 1869 and originally used for the (17) ______ (SPEED) transportation of tea between China and England. However, the (18) ______ (CONSTRUCT) of steam ships and the opening of the Suez Canal, which was (19) ______ (PASS) for such ships as the Cutty Sark, stole some of her initial glory.

She was not a strong (20) ______ (COMPETE) in the Sea races from China to England but later, between 1885 and 1895, she was (21) ______ (RIVAL) for speed as she carried wool between Melbourne and New York.

After a long working history and several changes in (22) ______ (OWNER), the ship was (23) ______ (LOVE) restored in the fifties and a dry dock was built so that the Cutty Sark could be used as a museum. (24) ______ (DONATE) are once again being requested as there is urgent need of further work on the ship.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct transformed word in each gap.

questions.instruction: 17. SPEED ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: SPEEDY. Danh từ 'SPEED' được biến đổi thành tính từ 'speedy' (nhanh chóng / cấp tốc) để bổ nghĩa cho danh từ 'transportation' đứng sau nó. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: used for the speedy transportation of
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 18. CONSTRUCT ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: CONSTRUCTION. Động từ 'CONSTRUCT' chuyển đổi thành danh từ chỉ hoạt động xây dựng/chế tạo 'construction' đứng sau mạo từ xác định 'the': 'the construction of steam ships'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Nouns.
questions.answerLocation: However, the construction of steam ships
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 19. PASS ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IMPASSABLE. Động từ 'PASS' chuyển thành tính từ 'passable', sau đó thêm tiền tố phủ định thành 'impassable' (không thể lưu thông/đi qua được). Vì Kênh đào Suez không có gió nên tàu buồm không thể đi qua, trong khi tàu hơi nước thì có thể. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Negative prefixes.
questions.answerLocation: which was impassable for such ships as
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 20. COMPETE ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: COMPETITOR. Động từ 'COMPETE' chuyển thành danh từ chỉ người/đối thủ tham gia cuộc thi 'competitor' đứng sau danh từ tính từ bổ nghĩa 'strong': 'she was not a strong competitor'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Nouns.
questions.answerLocation: She was not a strong competitor in the
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 21. RIVAL ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: UNRIVALLED (hoặc UNRIVALED). Danh từ/động từ 'RIVAL' được chuyển đổi thành tính từ phân từ bị động 'rivalled', sau đó thêm tiền tố phủ định thành 'unrivalled' (không có đối thủ / vô song) đứng sau hệ từ liên kết 'was': 'she was unrivalled for speed'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: she was unrivalled for speed as she carried
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 22. OWNER ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: OWNERSHIP. Danh từ chỉ người 'OWNER' được chuyển đổi bằng cách thêm hậu tố '-ship' thành danh từ trừu tượng chỉ quyền sở hữu 'ownership' đứng sau giới từ 'in': 'changes in ownership' (sự thay đổi quyền sở hữu). Chuyên đề: Word formation — Nouns.
questions.answerLocation: several changes in ownership, the ship
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 23. LOVE ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: LOVINGLY. Danh từ/động từ 'LOVE' chuyển thành tính từ 'loving' và thêm hậu tố trạng từ '-ly' thành 'lovingly' (một cách nâng niu/đầy yêu thương) để bổ nghĩa cho động từ phân từ bị động 'restored'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adverbs.
questions.answerLocation: the ship was lovingly restored in the
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 24. DONATE ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: DONATIONS. Động từ 'DONATE' được biến đổi thành danh từ 'donation'. Vì đứng làm chủ ngữ của động từ số nhiều 'are once again being requested', danh từ này phải ở dạng số nhiều viết hoa đầu câu: 'Donations'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Plural nouns.
questions.answerLocation: Donations are once again being requested
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: For questions 25-30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.
sections.passageHtml:
Part 4: Key Word Transformation

Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the key word in bold. Do not change the word given.

questionGroups.instruction: Complete the second sentence using the key word in bold.

questions.instruction: I’ve not seen such an exciting film for ages. (far) -> It was ____________________ have seen for ages.
acceptedAnswers: by far the most exciting film I
questions.correctAnswer: by far the most exciting film I
questions.explanation: Đáp án: BY FAR THE MOST EXCITING FILM I. Sử dụng trạng từ 'by far' đi kèm tính từ ở cấp độ so sánh nhất 'the most exciting' để nhấn mạnh mức độ vượt trội (phim thú vị nhất từ trước đến nay tôi từng xem). Chuyên đề: Modifiers & Superlatives. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: by far the most exciting film I
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: I felt totally worn out after a day’s gardening. (left) -> A day spent working in the garden ____________________ totally worn out.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: LEFT ME FEELING (hoặc LEFT ME). Cấu trúc 'leave someone + V-ing / adjective' nghĩa là 'khiến ai đó ở trong trạng thái như thế nào'. Ở đây quá khứ đơn là 'left me feeling totally worn out'. Chuyên đề: Verb patterns & Idiomatic verbs.
questions.answerLocation: left me feeling
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: The board of directors tried very hard to rescue the business from bankruptcy. (lengths) -> The board of directors ____________________ rescue the business from bankruptcy.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: WENT TO GREAT LENGTHS TO. Sử dụng thành ngữ cố định 'go to great lengths to do something' nghĩa là 'nỗ lực hết sức mình / làm mọi cách có thể để đạt mục tiêu'. Chuyên đề: Idioms & Fixed expressions.
questions.answerLocation: went to great lengths to
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: No one really knows what her reaction to the news of her promotion will be. (telling) -> There is ____________________ the news of her promotion.
acceptedAnswers: no telling how she will react to
no telling what her reaction will be to
questions.correctAnswer: no telling how she will react to
questions.explanation: Đáp án: NO TELLING HOW SHE WILL REACT TO (hoặc NO TELLING WHAT HER REACTION WILL BE TO). Cấu trúc thành ngữ khuyết thiếu 'There is no telling...' nghĩa là 'không thể biết trước được / không thể đoán được'. Kết hợp cấu trúc mệnh đề danh ngữ chỉ phản ứng đứng sau. Chuyên đề: Idioms & Noun clauses.
questions.answerLocation: no telling how she will react to
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: The race will be held indoors if it rains. (event) -> In the ____________________ the race will be held indoors.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IN THE EVENT OF RAIN (hoặc IN THE EVENT OF IT RAINING). Sử dụng cụm giới từ chỉ điều kiện giả định 'In the event of something' nghĩa là 'trong trường hợp xảy ra việc gì'. 'Rain' đóng vai trò danh từ hoặc danh động từ 'it raining'. Chuyên đề: Conditionals & Prepositional phrases.
questions.answerLocation: event of rain
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: No money can be refunded once the goods have been used. (entitled) -> Customers ____________________ once the goods have been used.
acceptedAnswers: are not entitled to a refund
questions.correctAnswer: are not entitled to a refund
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ARE NOT ENTITLED TO A REFUND. Sử dụng cụm cấu trúc bị động 'be entitled to something' nghĩa là 'có quyền được hưởng cái gì'. Ở đây phủ định là 'are not entitled to a refund' (khách hàng không có quyền nhận tiền hoàn trả). Chuyên đề: Passive structures & Fixed phrases.
questions.answerLocation: are not entitled to a refund
questions.source: Practice Test 6, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 5: Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article on tourism. For questions 31-36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
The Vanishing Tourist

One day, there will be no more tourists. There will be adventurers, fieldwork assistants, volunteers and, of course, travellers. There might still be those who quietly slip away to foreign lands for nothing more than pure pleasure, but it will be a secretive and frowned upon pursuit. No one will want to own up to being one of those. It might even be illegal.

Already tourists are discouraged from entering certain areas, and new names are being added to the list of territories where we should fear to tread. The charitable organisation Tourism Concern identifies seven countries as having areas that have been adversely affected by tourism. Tourists only wreak havoc. Tourists only destroy the natural environment. Tourists only emasculate local cultures. Tourists bring nothing with them but their money. They must be stopped at any price.

Yet less than 40 years ago, tourism was encouraged as an unquestionable good. With the arrival of the package holiday and charter flights, tourism could at last be enjoyed by the masses. By the 1980s, tourism was the largest and fastest-growing industry in the world and, by the end of the decade, 20 million Britons a year went abroad on holiday.

It won't be easy to wipe out this massive, ever growing tribe. Today there are more than 700m ‘tourist arrivals’ each year. The World Tourism Organisation forecasts that, by 2020, there will be 1.56 billion tourists travelling at any one time. The challenge to forcibly curtail more than a billion tourists from going where they want to go is so immense as to be impossible. You cannot make so many economically empowered people stop doing something they want to do unless you argue that it is of such extreme damage to the welfare of the world that only the truly malicious, utterly selfish and totally irresponsible would ever consider doing it. This is clearly absurd; whatever benefits or otherwise may accrue from tourism, it is not, despite what a tiny minority say, evil. It can cause harm. It can be morally neutral. And it can, occasionally, be a force for great good.

So the tourist is being attacked by more subtle methods: by being re-branded in the hope we won't recognise it as the unattractive entity it once was. The word ‘tourist’ is being removed from anything that was once called a holiday in the pamphlet that was once called a holiday brochure. Of course, adventurers, fieldwork assistants, volunteers and travellers don’t go on holidays. Un-tourists (as I will call them) go on things called ‘cultural experiences’, ‘expeditions’, ‘projects’, ‘mini-ventures’ and, most tellingly, ‘missions’. A Coral Cay Conservation Expedition flyer says: ‘The mission of any Coral Cay Conservation Volunteer is to help sustain livelihoods and alleviate poverty.’

The word mission is apposite. While this re-branding is supposed to present a progressive, modernistic approach to travel, in fact it is firmly rooted in the Victorian experience. Like Victorian travellers, the modern day un-tourist insists that the main motive behind their adventure is to help others. Whereas the mass tourist and the area they visit are condemned as anti-ethical and at loggerheads, the ethos of the un-tourist and the needs of the area they wander into are presumed to be in tune with each other. Environmental charity Earthwatch, which organises holidays for ‘volunteers’, assures that they will provide ‘life-changing opportunities for you and the environment ... See the world and give it a future.’

Un-tourists are very concerned about holding the moral high ground. Afraid of being tainted by association, they avoid identifiably tourist structures, such as hotels. They prefer to stay in a tent, a cabin, local-style houses such as yurts, thatched huts or, a typical example, ‘a traditional Malay wooden stilt house’. These, they believe, are somehow more in keeping with something they call local culture. Local culture is very important to the un-tourist, whereas the mass tourist is believed to both shun and obliterate it.

Un-tourism relies upon exclusivity; it is all about preventing other people travelling in order that you might legitimise your own travels. Pretending you are not doing something that you actually are — going on holiday — is at the heart of the un-tourist endeavour. Every aspect of the experience has to be disguised. Gone are the glossy brochures. Instead, the expeditions, projects and adventures are advertised in publications more likely to resemble magazines with a concern in ecological or cultural issues. The price is usually well hidden, as if there is a reluctance to admit that this is, in essence, a commercial transaction. There is something unedifying about having to pay to do good.

questionGroups.instruction: For questions 31-36, choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: 31. What does the writer say about tourists in the future?
options: A. they will take up other interests
B. they will be engaged in a banned activity
C. they will avoid being identified as tourists
D. their numbers will decline dramatically
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Tác giả dự báo ở đoạn 1: 'there will be no more tourists... but it will be a secretive and frowned upon pursuit. No one will want to own up to being one of those.' Điều này hàm ý trong tương lai, mọi người sẽ tránh bị nhận diện là khách du lịch đại trà do những định kiến và áp lực đạo đức xã hội. Bẫy: (B) sai vì du lịch có thể bị lên án chứ chưa chắc đã bất hợp pháp hoàn toàn, (D) sai vì thực tế số lượng người di chuyển vẫn tăng vọt dưới các danh nghĩa khác. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: No one will want to own up to being one of those.

questions.instruction: 32. What does the writer say in the second paragraph about current attitudes towards tourism?
options: A. that tourism is an activity that has to be discouraged
B. that tourists do not spend enough money
C. that tourists face dangers in certain parts of the world
D. that tourists are ignorant of local problems and customs
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Đoạn 2 ghi nhận luồng quan điểm hiện tại coi du lịch là tác nhân phá hoại môi trường và văn hóa, do đó cần phải ngăn chặn bằng mọi giá: 'Tourists only wreak havoc... They must be stopped at any price.' Điều này phản ánh rõ thái độ lên án và nỗ lực ngăn chặn du lịch đại trà. Bẫy: (B) ngược lại vì du lịch đem lại rất nhiều tiền nhưng bị coi là chỉ mang tiền chứ không đem lại lợi ích văn hóa, (C) và (D) không phải luận điểm chính của đoạn này. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: Tourists only wreak havoc... They must be stopped at any price.

questions.instruction: 33. Why will it not be easy to stop mass tourism?
options: A. It has been recognised and encouraged by the United Nations.
B. People will ignore warnings that what they are doing is morally wrong.
C. More people are enjoying the financial freedom to do as they please.
D. The number of tourists travelling the world is constantly increasing.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Tác giả lập luận rằng việc ngăn chặn du lịch cực kỳ khó khăn do quy mô nhân khẩu học quá lớn và đang tăng liên tục của tệp khách du lịch: 'Today there are more than 700m... WTO forecasts 1.56 billion... The challenge to forcibly curtail... is so immense as to be impossible.' Bẫy: (C) mặc dù tiền bạc giúp họ đi nhưng lý do chính khiến việc ngăn cản trở nên bất khả thi là do 'số lượng cực lớn và liên tục phát triển' của nhóm người này. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: The challenge to forcibly curtail more than a billion tourists from going where they want to go is so immense as to be impossible.

questions.instruction: 34. The writer quotes the Coral Cay conservation flyer in order to
options: A. emphasise the different nature of modern travel.
B. to illustrate the company’s desire to attract different customers.
C. to stress the point that holiday brochures are no longer produced.
D. to demonstrate the fact that these are merely holidays in disguise.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Việc trích dẫn flyer của Coral Cay nói về 'nhiệm vụ xóa đói giảm nghèo' thực chất là để vạch trần chiêu trò tiếp thị tái định dạng thương hiệu (re-branding). Bản chất của hoạt động này vẫn chỉ là một kỳ nghỉ du lịch thông thường nhưng được khoác lên tấm áo đạo đức 'làm việc tốt' nhằm xoa dịu lương tâm của du khách tránh mác 'tourist'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: Pretending you are not doing something that you actually are — going on holiday — is at the heart of the un-tourist endeavour.

questions.instruction: 35. In the penultimate paragraph, the writer suggests that un-tourists
options: A. fear being classed as ordinary tourists.
B. are antagonistic towards mass tourists.
C. have a desire to feel uncomfortable.
D. are often very interested in architecture.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Tác giả viết: 'Un-tourists are very concerned about holding the moral high ground. Afraid of being tainted by association, they avoid identifiably tourist structures...'. Điều này chứng minh động lực cốt lõi của họ là nỗi sợ bị quy chụp hay đánh đồng với tệp du khách thông thường, từ đó họ từ chối ở khách sạn mà chuyển sang lều trại. Chuyên đề: Reading — Implication.
questions.answerLocation: Afraid of being tainted by association, they avoid identifiably tourist structures

questions.instruction: 36. What does the writer say in the last paragraph about the price being hidden?
options: A. that the holidays are too expensive for most people’s budgets
B. that people would rather not think of it as paying for a holiday
C. that holidaymakers will be persuaded to pay a range of extra charges
D. that some of the money will go to businesses with unethical practices
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Việc che giấu mức giá trong quảng cáo du lịch bảo tồn bắt nguồn từ tâm lý e ngại thừa nhận đây chỉ là giao dịch thương mại mua bán kỳ nghỉ: 'as if there is a reluctance to admit that this is, in essence, a commercial transaction. There is something unedifying about having to pay to do good.' Người đi du lịch không muốn thừa nhận bản thân đang bỏ tiền mua một sản phẩm giải trí giả danh từ thiện. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: The price is usually well hidden, as if there is a reluctance to admit that this is, in essence, a commercial transaction.

sections.title: Part 6: Gapped Text
sections.instructions: You are going to read an extract from an article on sustainable forestry. Seven paragraphs have been removed from the extract. Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (37-43). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Part 6: Gapped Text

The fate of the rainforests is in our hands. Experts in tropical timber have concluded that developing countries lack either the will or the wherewithal to stop the onslaught which sees an area of rainforest the size of England destroyed each year.

[ Gap 37 ]

But as I discovered in a building project at my own home, putting conservation principles into practice is far from easy. For the past five months, my family has been enduring the misery that comes with the construction of a kitchen extension. When all the work is finished we will have a stylish modern space built with minimal environmental impact. The lightweight construction has highly-insulated walls and glazing which passes the eco-test; but it fails with the timber.

[ Gap 38 ]

The top timber certificate comes from the Forest Stewardship Council (FSC), which claims to ensure that the timber has been cut in a way that protects the environment and safeguards the conditions of workers. I clicked on their website and searched for patio doors. No mention of them. I phoned the main office and was referred back to the website. I phoned listed FSC timber merchants at random. There are very few of them. One told me that he could not inform me about FSC timber without knowing the joinery specification. The joiner said he could not specify sizes without knowing the qualities of the timber.

[ Gap 39 ]

I made an instant decision to put my faith in another FSC merchant, who assured me that some nice South American wood was being delivered that should fit the bill. But my brother warned that this particular timber was so oily that any paint applied to it would simply peel off.

[ Gap 40 ]

I chose softwood doors which would, I assumed, be made from Scandinavian timber. A month later a set of honey-coloured doors arrived. They seemed very hard for a softwood. The technical chap from the supplier explained that they used the term ‘softwood’ to mean wood that wasn’t the red colour people associated with hardwoods.

[ Gap 41 ]

As I sat in my new extension pondering my wasted effort, I stared at the walls and realised that I had ignored a far greater problem — the ply panels which line the walls and ceiling are made from tropical hardwood. They are not certified either and probably come from trees illegally cut.

[ Gap 42 ]

The department is trying to persuade a government body to take part in a pilot project with FSC timber, but it cannot find a volunteer. Government green procurement guidelines are said by consultants to be lax and open to abuse. Big refurbishments are under way at the Ministry of Defence, but these appear to have specified European wood - a policy which avoids controversy but does nothing for the sort of sustainable tropical forestry that the development department has been anxious to promote.

[ Gap 43 ]

Environmentalists say that councils will have to move more quickly on the issue if they are to reflect the concerns of their voters that action should be taken to preserve the rainforests — before it is too late.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (37-43).

questions.explanation: Đáp án H. Đoạn này tiếp tục phát triển quan điểm từ đoạn đầu tiên về việc bảo tồn rừng mưa nhiệt đới cần dựa vào sức mua của các quốc gia phương bắc giàu có ('purchasing power of the rich countries...'). Nó làm cầu nối tuyệt vời đến đoạn tiếp theo khi tác giả thu hẹp quy mô xuống dự án xây dựng cá nhân tại nhà mình: 'But as I discovered in a building project...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: But as I discovered in a building project at my own home

questions.explanation: Đáp án F. Đoạn F giới thiệu mục tiêu cụ thể của tác giả là tìm kiếm cánh cửa sân vườn (patio doors) không phá hủy rừng mưa nhiệt đới: 'My attention was focused on finding a set of patio doors...'. Nó kết nối trực tiếp với đoạn trước bàn về gỗ ('fails with the timber') và đoạn sau giới thiệu về chứng chỉ FSC cho cửa gỗ này: 'The top timber certificate comes from...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: I clicked on their website and searched for patio doors.

questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Đoạn B mô tả sự bế tắc khi các thương nhân bán gỗ ngừng cung cấp chứng chỉ FSC vì không ai mua và lịch trình xây dựng đang thúc ép: 'Time was ticking on the building schedule...'. Nó dẫn dắt mượt mà đến quyết định liều lĩnh của tác giả ở đoạn tiếp theo: 'I made an instant decision to put my faith in another FSC merchant...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: I made an instant decision to put my faith

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Đoạn A phản ánh việc tác giả phải đầu hàng trước áp lực thời tiết lạnh giá mùa đông và đứa con bị hắt hơi nên đã từ bỏ ý định mua gỗ cứng tự nhiên để chuyển sang catalogue gỗ mềm phổ thông: 'I abandoned the idea of certified hardwood...'. Đoạn tiếp theo xác nhận: 'I chose softwood doors which would...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: I chose softwood doors which would

questions.explanation: Đáp án G. Đoạn G làm rõ nguồn gốc thực tế của cánh cửa gỗ mềm vừa nhận được, hóa ra chúng được chế tạo bằng gỗ wawa của Ghana, không hề có chứng chỉ độc lập nào cả: 'The doors transpire to be wawa...'. Nó dẫn dắt trực tiếp sang sự thất vọng cay đắng ở đoạn kế tiếp: 'As I sat in my new extension pondering my wasted effort...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: As I sat in my new extension pondering my wasted effort

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Đoạn C mở rộng vấn đề từ quy mô gia đình nhỏ bé ra ngoài tầm vóc vĩ mô xã hội, tố cáo sự tắc trách của chính phủ trong các chính sách đầu tư xây dựng công: 'The tiny impact... is being replicated...'. Nó đứng ngay trước ví dụ thực tế về sự thất bại trong việc lôi kéo một cơ quan nhà nước thí điểm gỗ FSC: 'The department is trying to persuade... but it cannot find a volunteer.'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: The department is trying to persuade a government body

questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Đoạn E nối tiếp thông tin nỗ lực của các sở phát triển thúc đẩy lâm nghiệp bền vững ('helping the department do this') bằng việc kêu gọi Hiệp hội Kiến trúc sư Hoàng gia và chính quyền địa phương hành động. Đoạn tiếp theo nhắc tới các Hội đồng địa phương: 'Environmentalists say that councils will have to move...'. Chuyên đề: Reading — Cohesion & Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: Environmentalists say that councils will have to move more quickly

paragraphOptions.label: A
paragraphOptions.text: The winter chill was setting in and our sneezing three-year-old had begun to ask about the sheet of plastic hanging where the kitchen door should be. I abandoned the idea of certified hardwood and picked up a catalogue from a worldwide joinery supplier. The company’s environmental statement promises that care is taken of the environment wherever possible.

paragraphOptions.label: B
paragraphOptions.text: Time was ticking on the building schedule. Another timber merchant from the list told me he had stopped selling FSC timber because no one would buy it, even though he had priced it at the same level as regular timber. Builders did not recognise the types of wood in the yard, and architects had not specified it.

paragraphOptions.label: C
paragraphOptions.text: The tiny impact on rainforests of my kitchen extension is being replicated millions of times over across the developed world. The government says it is taking a lead in insisting that public buildings use wood from sustainable sources, but research by the environment department has failed to find any evidence that the policy is being carried out.

paragraphOptions.label: D
paragraphOptions.text: The living room had posed fewer problems when, four years ago, we decided to redecorate it and replace the door that leads out into the side garden. It was easy to find wood in a shade which was in keeping with the rest of the house and the colour scheme we chose for the walls turned out to be a very wise choice.

paragraphOptions.label: E
paragraphOptions.text: In a significant step towards helping the department do this, the Royal Institute for British Architects has committed itself to taking forest conservation seriously. But, even so, the hoardings that clad building sites are often ignored in the architects’ and planners’ specifications and come from unsustainable forests. Local authorities are also major buyers of timber, but a survey last week showed that only 27% of councils have a policy on sustainable wood.

paragraphOptions.label: F
paragraphOptions.text: My attention was focused on finding a set of patio doors that did not involve any rainforest destruction. My builder brother told me that, ideally, the doors should be made out of hardwood which would not twist or swell. Local oak is out of my price-range, so I resolved to buy doors made from wood certified as being from a sustainably-managed forest.

paragraphOptions.label: G
paragraphOptions.text: The doors transpire to be wawa, a hardwood from Ghana. They are not independently certified. A spokesman for the company said that although some of their products contain certified timber, supplies are too erratic to commit their entire manufacturing process to certified wood.

paragraphOptions.label: H
paragraphOptions.text: The only hope, say conservationists, lies with the purchasing power of the rich countries in the north. If we insist on timber that is sustainably harvested it will put pressure on the loggers to ply their trade with greater care. Western governments must act much more swiftly. So must local government and business.

sections.title: Part 7: Multiple Matching
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article about neuroplasticity and the science of language acquisition. For questions 44-53, choose from the scientists (A-E). The scientists may be chosen more than once.
sections.passageHtml:
Neuroplasticity and Language

How does the human brain adapt to the formidable task of mastering a new tongue? Five leading cognitive neuroscientists share their research on brain plasticity, age barriers, and the neural mechanics of language learning.

A. Dr. Clara Visser — The Critical Period Hypothesis

It has long been theorized that language learning is governed by a strict developmental timeline. The Critical Period Hypothesis suggests that there is a biologically determined window in early childhood when the brain is uniquely primed for language. As we age, global neuroplasticity naturally declines, making native-like second language acquisition exceptionally difficult for adults. However, my lab's research has thoroughly discredited the extreme view that adult brains have zero plasticity for phoneme learning. By utilizing intensive auditory training protocols, we demonstrated that the adult auditory cortex can be physically rewired to distinguish foreign phonemes, proving that plasticity is suppressed rather than entirely lost.

B. Dr. Hiroshi Tanaka — Cognitive Load and Neural Efficiency

Adult language learning is notorious for its heavy conscious cognitive load, relying heavily on the prefrontal cortex for explicit rule-following and translation. In stark contrast, children acquire their first language implicitly through subcortical structures like the basal ganglia, bypassing the burden of conscious grammatical analysis. Interestingly, as an adult student achieves high proficiency in a second language, we observe a dramatic structural phenomenon: the neural footprint of the second language dramatically shrinks, converging onto the same efficient, localized networks used by native speakers. This shift towards high neural efficiency shows that the brain optimizes its resources as fluency is consolidated.

C. Prof. Elena Rostova — The Bilingual Advantage and Executive Control

Managing two active language systems is an exceptional workout for the brain's executive control network. Because both languages remain perpetually active in a bilingual brain, the prefrontal cortex must constantly work to suppress the non-active language. This constant mental effort acts as a potent shield, building a massive cognitive reserve. Our longitudinal studies show that this cognitive reserve delayed the clinical onset of dementia and Alzheimer's symptoms in bilingual individuals by an average of four to five years compared to monolinguals, showing that the continuous effort of language management physically preserves brain health.

D. Dr. Julian Vance — Structural Remodeling and Cortical Density

Our focus is on the physical, anatomical remodeling that occurs during language study. Using high-resolution MRI scans, we proved that learning a new language increases grey matter density in the left inferior parietal cortex—the region crucial for integrating auditory and visual inputs. Amazingly, these structural modifications can occur within a remarkably brief timeframe of intensive linguistic immersion (in some cases, as little as six weeks). This rapid gray matter accumulation demonstrates that the adult brain's physical architecture is highly dynamic, actively reshaping its cellular networks in response to intense educational input.

E. Dr. Sophia Martinez — The Role of Emotion and Social Interaction

Many digital apps treat language as a purely computational task of vocabulary pattern recognition. However, real-world language acquisition is a deeply social and emotional experience. Human language learning relies heavily on the mirror neuron system, which fires when we observe and replicate the lip movements and facial expressions of others. Learning language through rich, face-to-face social interaction recruits emotional and social cognitive networks, triggering a dopamine release that drastically strengthens synaptic connections. This social priming makes vocabulary retention far superior compared to passive, screen-based digital input.

questionGroups.instruction: For questions 44-53, choose the correct scientist (A-E) using the separate answer sheet.

questions.instruction: 44. Mentions that physical structural modifications in the brain can occur within a remarkably brief timeframe of immersion.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Tiến sĩ Julian Vance chứng minh qua phim chụp cộng hưởng từ (MRI) rằng những thay đổi cấu trúc vật lý của não bộ có thể diễn ra trong một khoảng thời gian cực kỳ ngắn ngủi khi trải nghiệm môi trường ngôn ngữ chuyên sâu (trong vòng chưa đầy sáu tuần). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: these structural modifications can occur within a remarkably brief timeframe of intensive linguistic immersion (in some cases, as little as six weeks).

questions.instruction: 45. Identifies a cognitive benefit of bilingualism that manifests as a delay in age-related mental decline.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Giáo sư Elena Rostova chỉ ra lợi ích nhận thức của việc song ngữ là trì hoãn các triệu chứng suy giảm trí tuệ tuổi già (sa sút trí tuệ / Alzheimer) trung bình từ 4 đến 5 năm nhờ tích lũy được lượng dự trữ nhận thức khổng lồ. Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: delayed the clinical onset of dementia and Alzheimer's symptoms in bilingual individuals by an average of four to five years

questions.instruction: 46. Explains that children utilize a different, more implicit neural pathway for language acquisition compared to adults.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Tiến sĩ Hiroshi Tanaka giải thích sự khác biệt trong con đường thần kinh: trẻ em tiếp nhận ngôn ngữ một cách ngầm ẩn thông qua các cấu trúc dưới vỏ não (như hạch nền - basal ganglia), bỏ qua sự kiểm soát ý thức nặng nề ở thùy trước trán như người lớn. Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: children acquire their first language implicitly through subcortical structures like the basal ganglia, bypassing the burden of conscious

questions.instruction: 47. Challenges the assertion that the adult brain is entirely incapable of adapting to new speech sounds.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Tiến sĩ Clara Visser bác bỏ quan điểm cực đoan cho rằng não bộ người trưởng thành hoàn toàn không có tính mềm dẻo để học các âm vị tiếng nước ngoài ('discredited the extreme view that adult brains have zero plasticity for phoneme learning'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: discredited the extreme view that adult brains have zero plasticity for phoneme learning.

questions.instruction: 48. Connects language retention with the activation of emotional and social cognitive systems rather than pure digital input.
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Tiến sĩ Sophia Martinez nhấn mạnh việc học ngôn ngữ qua giao tiếp xã hội trực tiếp sẽ kích hoạt các mạng lưới cảm xúc và phát xạ dopamine, giúp củng cố liên kết synapse tốt hơn nhiều so với việc nạp dữ liệu số thụ động từ màn hình. Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: recruits emotional and social cognitive networks, triggering a dopamine release that drastically strengthens synaptic connections. This social priming makes vocabulary retention far superior

questions.instruction: 49. Mentions that the cognitive benefits of bilingualism arise from the continuous effort to inhibit one of the two languages.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Giáo sư Elena Rostova chỉ ra rằng lợi ích nhận thức này là hệ quả phụ của cơ chế não bộ liên tục phải hoạt động để ức chế ngôn ngữ không hoạt động ('prefrontal cortex must constantly work to suppress the non-active language'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: Because both languages remain perpetually active in a bilingual brain, the prefrontal cortex must constantly work to suppress the non-active language.

questions.instruction: 50. Describes how increased linguistic expertise is reflected in a reduced neural footprint and enhanced brain economy.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Tiến sĩ Hiroshi Tanaka mô tả hiện tượng khi học sinh đạt trình độ thông thạo cao, vùng hoạt động thần kinh cho ngôn ngữ thứ hai sẽ thu hẹp lại ('neural footprint of the second language dramatically shrinks'), tối ưu hóa hiệu năng tài nguyên của não bộ. Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: neural footprint of the second language dramatically shrinks, converging onto the same efficient, localized networks... shift towards high neural efficiency

questions.instruction: 51. Cites specific anatomical changes, such as increased grey matter density, as evidence of language-learning impact.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Tiến sĩ Julian Vance đưa ra bằng chứng giải phẫu học thực tế là sự gia tăng mật độ chất xám ở vỏ não thùy đỉnh dưới trái ('increases grey matter density in the left inferior parietal cortex') sau khi học ngôn ngữ mới. Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: proved that learning a new language increases grey matter density in the left inferior parietal cortex

questions.instruction: 52. Refers to a biologically determined developmental window outside of which native-like fluency is difficult to achieve.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Tiến sĩ Clara Visser đề cập đến giả thuyết Thời kỳ tới hạn chỉ ra một khung cửa sổ sinh học trẻ thơ khi não bộ được thiết kế đặc biệt tối ưu cho ngôn ngữ ('biologically determined window in early childhood when the brain is uniquely primed'). Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: Critical Period Hypothesis suggests that there is a biologically determined window in early childhood

questions.instruction: 53. Emphasizes the importance of the mirror neuron system in human-to-human language imitation.
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Tiến sĩ Sophia Martinez giải thích vai trò của hệ thống neuron gương phản chiếu chuyển động môi và biểu cảm khuôn mặt của người khác trong quá trình bắt chước học hỏi ngôn ngữ trực tiếp. Chuyên đề: Reading — Multiple Matching.
questions.answerLocation: relies heavily on the mirror neuron system, which fires when we observe and replicate the lip movements and facial expressions of others.
